page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, which classifies it as a memory-optimized configuration. It includes 2835 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. This resource density provides a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads, offering high qualitative cost efficiency by consolidating large databases and enterprise applications onto a single node without requiring external storage overhead.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks (3532.63 GB/sec) and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression (35.53 GB/sec). Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU scores are also strong, alongside high throughput in Redis SET operations (76.28 million operations/sec) and static web serving (61.11 GB/sec). While LLM prompt processing is strong for small and medium models, text generation for large 70B models is weak. This profile makes the server highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data compression pipelines, and general enterprise workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, backed by a 480 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and 2835 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong results in small-block memory bandwidth, Passmark CPU tests, and Redis SET operations. However, LLM text generation for large models remains a performance bottleneck. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, in-memory caching, and data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, built for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local storage, and strong database and caching performance.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM for memory-intensive applications
Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in Redis SET operations and small-block memory bandwidth
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data pipelines
